chief_complaint: A 64.33-year-old man (9 men and 3 women) with ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) who had just returned to continuous spontaneous circulation via cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR)
history: The patient had a history of STEMI with anterior wall elevation on electrocardiography (ECG), and CPR was performed for an unknown duration. The patient received thrombolytic therapy at 3 hospitals between April 2007 and February 2021.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG showed anterior wall elevation in 66.7% and inferior wall elevation in 33.3% of patients. CPR duration was associated with a higher incidence of rib fractures but not bleeding complications.
correct_diagnosis: ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) with return of spontaneous circulation
differentials: ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) with return of spontaneous circulation
correct_ranking: ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) with return of spontaneous circulation
